question: Did the model explicitly and unambiguously select Option B as the correct missing piece?
Did the model use specific visual features such as the 'GENESIS LABS' logo, shelving, or floor hazard stripes to justify the fit of Option B?
Did the model rule out Option A by noting semantic inconsistencies (e.g., it contains the pink-haired character who is already fully visible on the left) or orientation mismatch?
Did the model rule out Options C and D by noting they depict the wrong region (e.g., lower legs/floor only) or are rotated?
If a reconstructed image was generated, is the selected piece (Option B) placed correctly in the central white rectangular area?

In the generated reconstructed image, do the geometric features (floor lines, hazard stripes) and character parts (legs) connect smoothly across the border of the inserted piece without significant offsets?
Does the inserted piece in the generated image match the scale, perspective, and artistic style of the original background without significant artifacts?
Does the final reconstructed image visually depict a coherent scene with the two central standing characters and the 'GENESIS LABS' background as seen in Option B?
If intermediate images were generated, do they faithfully reflect the content of the options without hallucinating new objects or details not present in the original images?
If the text describes specific connecting features (e.g., legs connecting to feet, stripes aligning), are these connections visually verifiable in the generated image?
Are there any contradictions between the text explanation and the visual evidence in the generated images (e.g., text says 'perfect fit' but image shows misalignment)?
